SAMPLE OFFICE BUILDING
Project No.: TEST-2026-001
Location: 500 Main Street, Anytown, USA
Owner: Test Development Corp
Bid Date: June 1, 2026

PROJECT DESCRIPTION
New two-story office building, approximately 10,000 square feet. Scope includes
cast-in-place concrete foundations, structural steel framing, and interior finishes.

================================================================================
SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE
================================================================================

PART 1 - GENERAL

1.1 SUMMARY
A. Cast-in-place concrete for foundations, slab-on-grade, and columns.

1.2 QUALITY ASSURANCE
A. Concrete compressive strength: 3,000 PSI minimum at 28 days.
B. Reinforcing steel: ASTM A615, Grade 60, deformed bars.

PART 2 - PRODUCTS

2.1 CONCRETE MIX
A. Normal-weight concrete, unit weight 145 pcf.
B. Maximum w/c ratio: 0.50.

PART 3 - EXECUTION

3.1 QUANTITIES
A. Spread footings and grade beams: 120 CY, 3,000 PSI.
B. Slab-on-grade: 200 CY at 4-inch thickness over compacted fill.
C. Columns (4 each, 12-inch square): 15 CY, 4,000 PSI.
Total concrete: 335 CY.

3.2 FORMWORK AND PLACEMENT
A. All formwork per ACI 347. Strip after minimum 70% design strength achieved.
B. Cure all flatwork minimum 7 days with curing compound or wet burlap.

================================================================================
SECTION 05 12 00 - STRUCTURAL STEEL FRAMING
================================================================================

PART 1 - GENERAL

1.1 SUMMARY
A. Structural steel wide-flange columns, beams, and girders for two-story frame.

1.2 SUBMITTALS
A. Shop drawings by licensed structural engineer.
B. Mill certifications for all sections.

PART 2 - PRODUCTS

2.1 STEEL MATERIALS
A. Wide-flange shapes: ASTM A992, Grade 50.
B. Bolts: ASTM A325 high-strength.
C. Welding electrodes: AWS E70XX per D1.1.

PART 3 - EXECUTION

3.1 QUANTITIES
A. Columns (8 each, W8x31): approximately 12 tons.
B. Floor and roof beams and girders: approximately 18 tons.
C. Miscellaneous steel (angles, plates, connections): 3 tons.
Total structural steel: 33 tons.

3.2 ERECTION
A. All work by AISC-certified fabricator and erector.
B. Final plumb within L/500 of member length.

================================================================================
SECTION 09 91 00 - PAINTING
================================================================================

PART 1 - GENERAL

1.1 SUMMARY
A. Interior painting of gypsum board walls, ceilings, and miscellaneous metals.

1.2 QUALITY ASSURANCE
A. Applicator: minimum 3 years' experience with specified products.
B. Comply with all local VOC regulations.

PART 2 - PRODUCTS

2.1 PAINT SYSTEMS
A. Interior primer: latex-based, alkali-resistant.
B. Interior finish, walls: two coats semi-gloss latex.
C. Interior finish, ceilings: two coats flat latex.
D. Metal primer: rust-inhibiting alkyd primer, one coat.

PART 3 - EXECUTION

3.1 QUANTITIES
A. Gypsum board walls: 18,000 SF, prime plus two finish coats.
B. Exposed ceilings: 10,000 SF, one prime plus two finish coats.
C. Door frames and miscellaneous metals: 60 frames plus miscellaneous items.

3.2 SURFACE PREPARATION
A. All surfaces clean, dry, and free of contamination before application.
B. Sand and fill all fastener dimples and joints before priming.

================================================================================
END OF SPECIFICATIONS
================================================================================

NOTE FOR GAP ANALYSIS:
The following standard CSI divisions are intentionally omitted from this
abbreviated specification and should be flagged during gap analysis:
- Division 07 (Thermal and Moisture Protection — roofing not included)
- Division 08 (Openings — no door or window specs provided)
- Division 23 (HVAC — mechanical systems not specified)
- Division 26 (Electrical — electrical systems not specified)
